title: Solving Quadratic Equations by Taking Square Roots

blocks.body: If x^2 = k, then x = sqrt(k) or x = -sqrt(k) (written as x = +/- sqrt(k)). This method works best when there is no x-term (b = 0).

blocks.label: Example 1
blocks.body: Solve x^2 = 49. x = +/- 7. Solutions: x = 7 and x = -7.
If k < 0 (e.g., x^2 = -9), there is no real solution because no real number squared gives a negative number.
